enonce.texte: Match the shopping vocabulary words with their correct meanings. Write the letter of the correct answer next to the number.

sousQuestions.texte: A. The standard of something (good or bad)
B. The amount of money needed to buy something
C. Food and other items bought at a supermarket
D. The amount of money you pay for something

enonce.texte: Classify the following nouns as countable or uncountable. Write 'C' for countable and 'U' for uncountable.

questions.texte: Classify these nouns: apples, rice, bread, oranges, milk, eggs
questions.reponse: apples (C), rice (U), bread (U), oranges (C), milk (U), eggs (C)

sousQuestions.reponse: U (uncountable - you cannot count rice grains easily)
U (uncountable - bread is considered uncountable in English)

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct partitive expression from the box.

Box: a bottle of, a kilo of, a piece of, a loaf of, a cup of, a packet of

questions.texte: Complete: 'I would like ______ bread for breakfast.'
Complete: 'She bought ______ rice at the market.'
Complete: 'Please buy ______ sugar from the shop.'

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue between a customer and a shop assistant. Fill in the blanks with the correct shopping vocabulary from the box.

Box: price, cost, quality, groceries, clothes, shoes

questions.texte:
Complete the dialogue:

Customer: Excuse me, how much do these ______ cost? (items you wear on your feet)
Assistant: These shoes are 5000 CFA.
Customer: What about the ______ of this dress? (how much it is)
Assistant: The dress is 8000 CFA. The ______ is very good. (the standard)
Customer: I also need some ______ for the week. (food items)

intitule: Exercise 5: Identifying Countable and Uncountable Nouns

enonce.texte:
Read the shopping list below. Identify the countable and uncountable nouns and write them in the correct columns.

Shopping List:

  • 2 apples
  • 1 bottle of oil
  • 3 oranges
  • 1 kilo of sugar
  • bread
  • milk
  • eggs
  • rice
  • cheese
  • tomatoes

questions.texte: Classify the items into countable and uncountable nouns.
questions.reponse:
Countable: apples, oranges, eggs, tomatoes
Uncountable: oil, sugar, bread, milk, rice, cheese

sousQuestions.reponse: oil, sugar, bread, milk, rice, cheese

enonce.texte: You are going to the supermarket to buy items for your family. Write a shopping list with 10 items. For each item, specify the quantity and whether it is countable or uncountable. Use partitives where necessary.

questions.reponse:
Example: 1. 2 apples (countable)
2. a bottle of milk (uncountable)
3. 1 kilo of rice (uncountable)
4. 6 eggs (countable)
5. a loaf of bread (uncountable)
6. 3 oranges (countable)
7. a packet of sugar (uncountable)
8. tomatoes (countable)
9. cheese (uncountable)
10. a bottle of oil (uncountable)

enonce.texte: Write questions and answers for the following shopping situations. Use the vocabulary from the lesson.

questions.texte: You want to know the price of a pair of shoes. What do you ask?
You want to know if the quality of a dress is good. What do you ask?
questions.reponse: Is the quality of this dress good?

questions.texte: You want to buy 2 kilos of rice. How do you say it?
questions.reponse: I would like 2 kilos of rice, please.

questions.texte: You want to know the cost of a new phone. What do you ask?
questions.reponse: How much does this phone cost?

enonce.texte: Imagine you are at the market. Write a short dialogue between you (the customer) and a seller. Use at least 5 shopping vocabulary words from the lesson.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between a customer and a seller.
questions.reponse:
Example:
Customer: Good morning! How much are these oranges?
Seller: Good morning! They are 500 CFA per kilo. The quality is very good.
Customer: I would like 2 kilos, please. And what is the price of these tomatoes?
Seller: Tomatoes are 300 CFA per kilo.
Customer: I also need some groceries. How much does a loaf of bread cost?
Seller: A loaf of bread costs 400 CFA.
Customer: Thank you! I will take everything.
